name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is an easy-to-learn, high-level programming language known for its readability and versatility. It is widely used for web development, scripting, automation, data science, and more. Python emphasizes code clarity and allows programmers to express concepts in fewer lines of code.
usedFor: Web development, scripting, automation, data analysis, artificial intelligence, machine learning, scientific computing, and backend services.
funFact: Python is named after the television series 'Monty Python’s Flying Circus,' not the snake.

examples.title: Print Hello World
examples.beginnerExplanation: This line tells the computer to show the message Hello, world! on the screen.
explanation: print('Hello, world!') - This command outputs the text inside the quotes to the screen.

examples.title: Add Two Numbers
examples.beginnerExplanation: This code sets two values and shows their total.
explanation: a = 5      # Store the number 5 in a variable named a
b = 7      # Store the number 7 in a variable named b
print(a + b)   # Add a and b, then print the result (12)

examples.title: Loop through List
examples.beginnerExplanation: This code goes through a list of fruit and prints each one.
explanation: for fruit in ['apple', 'banana', 'cherry']:  # Go through each word in the list

websites.title: Python Official Documentation
websites.description: Comprehensive and official resource for Python's syntax and features.

websites.title: Real Python
websites.description: Guides and tutorials for learning Python programming effectively.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is the most widely used language for building interactive websites and web applications. It allows developers to create dynamic content and control web page behavior. JavaScript can now also be used on the server side and for mobile apps.
usedFor: Web development (frontend and backend), server-side scripting, mobile app development, browser extensions, and game development.
funFact: JavaScript was created in only 10 days and was originally called Mocha.

examples.title: Print Message
examples.beginnerExplanation: This code tells the computer to display Hello, world! in the console (a special output box).
explanation: console.log('Hello, world!');  // Show the text in the console

examples.title: Change Web Page Text
examples.beginnerExplanation: This changes everything on a web page to say Welcome!
explanation: document.body.innerHTML = 'Welcome!';  // Set the main content of the page to 'Welcome!'

examples.title: Add Two Numbers
examples.beginnerExplanation: This puts two numbers together and shows their sum.
explanation: let a = 3;       // Create a variable named a with value 3
let b = 4;       // Create a variable named b with value 4
console.log(a + b); // Add a and b, then print the result (7)

websites.title: JavaScript MDN Guide
websites.description: The standard reference for all things JavaScript from Mozilla.

websites.title: JavaScript.info
websites.description: A modern and detailed tutorial for learning JavaScript step by step.

name: TypeScript
creator: Anders Hejlsberg (Microsoft)
description: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds static typing and advanced features. It helps developers catch errors early and build scalable, reliable applications. TypeScript code is converted to JavaScript to run in browsers or servers.
usedFor: Large-scale web applications, backend services, and modern frontend frameworks like Angular and React.
funFact: TypeScript was developed by the same creator as the C# programming language.

examples.title: Type Annotation Example
examples.beginnerExplanation: This code creates a message and prints it, with a note that the message is a sentence (string).
explanation: let message: string = 'Hello, TypeScript!';  // Create a variable called message, and tell TypeScript it will be text
console.log(message);  // Print the message to the console

examples.title: Function with Types
examples.beginnerExplanation: This creates a function (like a small machine) that adds two numbers.
explanation: function add(a: number, b: number): number { // Create a function named add that takes 2 numbers
return a + b;                             // Add the numbers and return the sum
console.log(add(2, 3)); // Call the function and print the answer (5)

examples.title: Interface Example
examples.beginnerExplanation: This code creates a blueprint for a person, then makes one named Ada, age 22.
explanation: interface Person {           // Make a blueprint called Person
name: string;              // Each person has a name
age: number;               // And has an age
const user: Person = { name: 'Ada', age: 22 }; // Make a user from the blueprint

websites.title: TypeScript Handbook

websites.title: TypeScript Tutorial
websites.description: Beginner-friendly lessons and code samples.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is a foundational systems programming language popular for its speed and direct control over hardware. It is used to write operating systems, embedded software, and performance-critical apps. C has influenced countless modern languages.
usedFor: Operating systems, device drivers, embedded systems, compilers, databases, and hardware programming.
funFact: The UNIX operating system and much of Linux are written in C.

name: C++

description: C++ builds on C with object-oriented and generic features, allowing for complex and efficient software. Renowned for its performance and versatility, C++ powers many large-scale applications and games.
usedFor: Game engines, desktop applications, high-performance systems, financial software, and 3D graphical applications.
funFact: C++ enabled the creation of major game engines like Unreal Engine and Unity’s core.

name: Go
creator: Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, Ken Thompson (Google)
description: Go, or Golang, is a statically typed language developed at Google. Its simplicity, speed, and ease of concurrent programming make it perfect for modern cloud and server applications.
usedFor: Cloud services, web servers, command line tools, networking, and scalable backend systems.
funFact: Go's mascot is called the 'Gopher', created by Renée French.

import "fmt"
func main() {

    b := 5
    fmt.Println(a + b)
}

usedFor: Android mobile apps, web servers, enterprise software, banking systems, and scientific tools.
funFact: Java's original name was 'Oak,' after an oak tree outside Gosling's window.

name: Assembly
creator: Kathleen Booth
description: Assembly language is the lowest level human-readable programming language, offering direct control of the computer's hardware. Each assembly language is specific to a computer architecture.
usedFor: Embedded systems, operating systems, device drivers, and performance-critical hardware tasks.
funFact: Early video game consoles and computers often required developers to write in assembly for speed.

examples.title: Hello World (x86, NASM)
examples.code:
section .data
    msg db 'Hello, world!',0Ah
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov edx,13
    mov ecx,msg
    mov ebx,1
    mov eax,4
    int 0x80
    mov eax,1
    int 0x80
examples.beginnerExplanation: This low-level code tells a computer to print Hello, world! in a special way.
explanation: msg db 'Hello, world!',0Ah ; Store the message

examples.title: Add Numbers (x86, NASM)
examples.code:
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov eax,5
    add eax,3
    mov ebx,0
    mov eax,1
    int 0x80
examples.beginnerExplanation: This adds two numbers, but does not show the result (as that is complex in assembly).

examples.title: Simple Loop (x86, NASM)
examples.code:
section .data
    num db 0
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov ecx,3
loop_start:
    dec ecx
    jns loop_start
    mov eax,1
    int 0x80
examples.beginnerExplanation: This repeats a step 3 times then ends.
explanation: jns loop_start     ; If not negative, repeat loop

websites.title: PC Assembly Language
websites.description: A comprehensive guide to x86 assembly by Paul Carter.

websites.title: OnlineGDB Assembly
websites.description: Web-based assembler for trying simple code online.

name: BASIC
creator: John G. Kemeny, Thomas E. Kurtz
description: BASIC (Beginner's All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code) is a family of easy-to-learn languages designed for simplicity. Once the main language of home computers, it helped teach millions how to program.
usedFor: Educational software, retro games, and automation on vintage computers.
funFact: Microsoft's very first product was a BASIC interpreter developed for the Altair 8800.

examples.title: Hello World
examples.beginnerExplanation: This tells the computer to show Hello, world! on the screen.
explanation: PRINT "Hello, world!"    ' Show the phrase in quotes

examples.title: Add Numbers
examples.beginnerExplanation: This puts two numbers together and prints the answer.
explanation: PRINT A + B       ' Add and print the result (7)

examples.title: Simple Loop
examples.code:
FOR I = 1 TO 3
  PRINT I
NEXT I
examples.beginnerExplanation: This shows the numbers 1, 2, and 3 one by one.
explanation: FOR I = 1 TO 3    ' Start a loop from 1 to 3

name: Lua

description: Lua is a lightweight and easy-to-embed scripting language, designed to be efficient and flexible. It is often used as an embedded scripting tool in games and applications.
usedFor: Game scripting (Roblox, World of Warcraft), embedded firmware, and rapid prototyping.
funFact: Roblox, one of the world's biggest user-generated game platforms, depends on Lua for its scripting.

usedFor: Web development, automation, scripting, and web applications (especially startups).
funFact: Ruby was influenced by languages like Perl, Smalltalk, Eiffel, and Lisp, blending their best ideas.

name: Bash

usedFor: System administration, task automation, server management, scripting, and data processing.
funFact: Every command you run in your terminal can be stored in history and easily repeated using Bash.
